yafs.stats¶

class yafs.stats.Metrics(default_results_path: str | None = None)[source]¶

CSV writer used by yafs.core.Sim.

The class keeps the public contract historically used by core.py: insert records event-level rows and insert_link records hop-level rows. The CSV schema is unchanged so older analysis scripts remain valid.

EVENT_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'app', 'module', 'message', 'DES.src', 'DES.dst', 'TOPO.src', 'TOPO.dst', 'module.src', 'service', 'time_in', 'time_out', 'time_emit', 'time_reception')¶
LINK_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'src', 'dst', 'app', 'latency', 'message', 'ctime', 'size', 'buffer')¶
TIME_LATENCY = 'time_latency'¶
TIME_RESPONSE = 'time_response'¶
TIME_SERVICE = 'time_service'¶
TIME_TOTAL_RESPONSE = 'time_total_response'¶
TIME_WAIT = 'time_wait'¶
WATT_SERVICE = 'byService'¶
WATT_UPTIME = 'byUptime'¶
close() → None[source]¶
flush() → None[source]¶
insert(value: Dict[str, Any]) → None[source]¶
insert_link(value: Dict[str, Any]) → None[source]¶
class yafs.stats.MetricsAnalyzer(defaultPath: str = 'result', app_definition: Any | None = None, event_df: DataFrame | None = None, link_df: DataFrame | None = None)[source]¶

Rich metrics reader and analyser for the CSV files generated by YAFS.

Parameters¶

defaultPath:

Base path for <defaultPath>.csv and <defaultPath>_link.csv.

app_definition:

Optional app/service definition source. It can be a file path, a JSON object, or a list of dictionaries with the same structure as appDefinition.json.

event_df / link_df:

Optional preloaded dataframes, mainly useful for testing.

EVENT_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'app', 'module', 'message', 'DES.src', 'DES.dst', 'TOPO.src', 'TOPO.dst', 'module.src', 'service', 'time_in', 'time_out', 'time_emit', 'time_reception')¶
LINK_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'src', 'dst', 'app', 'latency', 'message', 'ctime', 'size', 'buffer')¶
application_cost_breakdown(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]¶

Backwards-compatible alias for execution-based cost breakdown.

application_distance_breakdown(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]¶

Aggregate request distance by application.

application_execution_cost_breakdown(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]¶

Compute execution cost per application from service time and node COST.

average_application_response_latency(app_name: Any | None = None, strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]¶

Return mean response latency per application.

average_loop_response(time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]]) → List[float][source]¶
average_messages_not_transmitted() → float[source]¶
bytes_transmitted() → float[source]¶
compute_times_df() → None[source]¶
count_messages() → int[source]¶
estimate_egress_cost(node_regions: Any, cost_per_gb: float, app_name: Any | None = None, by_request: bool = False) → DataFrame[source]¶

Estimate inter-region egress cost.

Notes¶

This requires extra region metadata. node_regions can be a {node_name: region} mapping or a topology JSON file with clusters[].region and clusters[].nodes[].name.

get_cost_cloud(topology: Any)[source]¶
get_df_modules() → DataFrame[source]¶
get_df_service_utilization(service: str, time: float) → DataFrame[source]¶
get_service_definition(app_name: Any) → ServiceDefinition | None[source]¶
get_watt(totaltime: float, topology: Any, by: str = 'byService')[source]¶
link_breakdown(groupby: Any = ('src', 'dst'), app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]¶
link_utilization(topology: Any, include_unused: bool = False) → DataFrame[source]¶

Compute utilization for each physical link in the topology.

mean_topology_congestion(topology: Any) → float[source]¶
message_breakdown(groupby: Any = ('app', 'message'), app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]¶
message_instances() → DataFrame[source]¶

Return one row per (app, id, message) combining event and link data.

messages_not_transmitted() → Series[source]¶
observation_window() → float[source]¶

Return the observed simulation window covered by the metrics traces.

peak_messages_not_transmitted() → float[source]¶
request_breakdown(app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]¶
request_distance_breakdown(topology: Any, app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]¶

Return the total traversed distance per request in kilometres.

request_hops(app_name: Any | None = None) → DataFrame[source]¶

Return the number of hops traversed by each request.

service_path_breakdown(app_name: Any | None = None, include_return_messages: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]¶
service_paths(app_name: Any | None = None, include_return_messages: bool = True) → Dict[str, List[Tuple[str, ...]]][source]¶
service_response(app_name: Any | None = None, strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]¶
showLoops(time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]]) → List[float][source]¶
showResults(total_time: float, topology: Any, time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]] | None = None) → None[source]¶
showResults2(total_time: float, time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]] | None = None) → None[source]¶
summarize_application_cost(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]¶

Backwards-compatible alias for execution-based application cost.

summarize_application_execution_cost(topology: Any) → DataFrame[source]¶
summarize_application_execution_metrics(topology: Any, strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True, node_regions: Any | None = None, egress_cost_per_gb: float | None = None) → DataFrame[source]¶

Build one summary row per application combining response and cost.

summarize_application_metrics(topology: Any, strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True, node_regions: Any | None = None, egress_cost_per_gb: float | None = None) → DataFrame[source]¶

Backwards-compatible alias for execution-based application metrics.

summarize_service_response(app_name: Any | None = None, strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]¶
times(time: str, value: str = 'mean') → DataFrame[source]¶
total_bandwidth_available(topology: Any) → float[source]¶
total_bandwidth_used(topology: Any) → float[source]¶
total_links(topology: Any) → int[source]¶
utilization(id_entity: Any, total_time: float, from_time: float = 0.0) → float[source]¶
valueLoop(total_time: float, time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]] | None = None) → float | None[source]¶
class yafs.stats.Stats(defaultPath: str = 'result', app_definition=None)[source]¶

Backward-compatible metrics reader.

Stats now delegates to yafs.metrics.MetricsAnalyzer, keeping the historical API while exposing richer analysis helpers from the new metrics layer.

EVENT_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'app', 'module', 'message', 'DES.src', 'DES.dst', 'TOPO.src', 'TOPO.dst', 'module.src', 'service', 'time_in', 'time_out', 'time_emit', 'time_reception')¶
LINK_COLUMNS = ('id', 'type', 'src', 'dst', 'app', 'latency', 'message', 'ctime', 'size', 'buffer')¶
application_cost_breakdown(topology: Any) → DataFrame¶

Backwards-compatible alias for execution-based cost breakdown.

application_distance_breakdown(topology: Any) → DataFrame¶

Aggregate request distance by application.

application_execution_cost_breakdown(topology: Any) → DataFrame¶

Compute execution cost per application from service time and node COST.

average_application_response_latency(app_name: Any | None = None, strategy: str = 'critical_path', include_return_messages: bool = True) → DataFrame¶

Return mean response latency per application.

average_loop_response(time_loops: Sequence[Sequence[str]]) → List[float]¶
average_messages_not_transmitted() → float¶
bytes_transmitted() → float¶
compute_times_df() → None¶
count_messages() → int¶
estimate_egress_cost(node_regions: Any, cost_per_gb: float, app_name: Any | None = None, by_request: bool = False) → DataFrame¶

Estimate inter-region egress cost.

Notes¶

This requires extra region metadata. node_regions can be a {node_name: region} mapping or a topology JSON file with clusters[].region and clusters[].nodes[].name.
